title: G002: Implement Keystone Chainsaw E2E test suite
summary: Review of commit d8179dc addressing all 8 findings from external reviewer berendt. All feedback items are correctly implemented: unique database names per test (W-001), execution-vs-display-order clarification in docs (W-002/I-003), exact scale-down assertion (W-003), anchored grep pattern (W-004), updatedReplicas rollout check (B-001), MariaDB CR cleanup assertions (I-001), and reason field in SecretsReady assertion (I-002). Documentation is consistent with implementation changes. No new issues introduced.

tests_checklist.item: All 9 E2E test directories exist under tests/e2e/keystone/ with chainsaw-test.yaml and fixture YAML files
All YAML files begin with SPDX Apache-2.0 header
All chainsaw-test.yaml files use apiVersion chainsaw.kyverno.io/v1alpha1 kind Test
All condition assertions use JMESPath conditions[?type == 'X'] syntax
All 9 Keystone CRs have unique names (no name collisions)
All 9 Keystone CRs have unique database names (no shared schema for parallel execution)
Each test uses extended assert timeout (5m) for full reconciliation
Error checks in deletion-cleanup include MariaDB CRs (Database, User, Grant)
image-upgrade uses anchored grep (2025\.2$) and updatedReplicas == replicas

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing Chainsaw test patterns from infra-stack-health and invalid-cr
Comments explain non-obvious decisions (MariaDB CR owner references)
No dead code or stale references to old shared database name

security_checklist.item: No hardcoded secrets in test fixtures
No sensitive data exposure in script steps

architecture_checklist.item: Database isolation per test suite prevents concurrent db_sync conflicts

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No unnecessary additions beyond reviewer feedback scope

fail_fast_checklist.item: Exact equality assertion catches scale-down failures early
Anchored grep prevents false-positive stale state matches
updatedReplicas check proves rollout completion rather than relying on stale pods

suggested_improvements: INFO: tests/e2e/keystone/scale/chainsaw-test.yaml:48,65 — Scale-up assertions use (availableReplicas >= N) while scale-down (line 82) uses exact match. The asymmetry is semantically justified (>= for scale-up correctly waits until enough pods are available; exact match for scale-down proves pods were actually terminated). Consider adding a brief comment at line 82 explaining why exact match is used here but not for scale-up, to prevent future reviewers from 'fixing' the asymmetry.
